turns.name: LEVINE
turns.text: Because it seems to me that in the basic case, and virtually all cases, where a historical fact — a what-happenea Rind of fact — is susceptible to an inference that is reasonable ana apparent, the reviewing court, whether it — whether the mental process it goes through is, "I'm exercising my own judgment" or "I'm deferring to the jury's finaing" is in 99 percent of the cases going to come up with the exact same ceclsfon. But if you — if you start going oown tne Line where you defer to the jury findings or what the jury could have founo on inferences, you're going to eliminate the process of judgment.
